entries.title: Submitting a Prayer Request
sampleQuestions: How can I submit a prayer request?
Can someone who does not attend CrossPointe request prayer?
Will the staff pray for me?
When does the staff pray over requests?
requiredFacts: Anyone may submit a prayer request, including someone who does not attend CrossPointe.
Everyone who submits a request is added to the prayer list.
CrossPointe staff meets weekly to pray over the prayer list and believes in the power of prayer.
requiredActions: Affirm the importance of prayer without promising a particular outcome.

entries.title: Prayer Request Privacy and Prayer Wall
sampleQuestions: Will my prayer request be private?
Can I submit a prayer request anonymously?
Who can see my prayer request?
What is the Prayer Wall?
requiredFacts: The online Prayer Request Form collects the person's name and email address.
In-person prayer requests may be submitted anonymously.
Prayer requests remain with CrossPointe staff unless the person chooses to post the request publicly on the Prayer Wall.
The Prayer Request Form allows someone to mark a request private.
requiredActions: Encourage people not to include highly sensitive medical, legal, financial, or identifying details.
prohibitedInformation: Sensitive medical, legal, or financial information

entries.title: Prayer Request Follow-Up
sampleQuestions: Will someone contact me after I submit a prayer request?
Can I ask a pastor to follow up about my prayer request?
How do I request a response from the church?
requiredFacts: The Prayer Request Form allows someone to request pastoral follow-up.
Someone should expect a response only when they request follow-up.
A person requesting follow-up may be referred to a pastor or scheduled for an appointment.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

entries.title: Pastoral Support and Conversations
sampleQuestions: Can I speak with a pastor?
Will a pastor visit someone in the hospital?
Can the church help me with grief?
Can I talk to a pastor about marriage problems?
requiredFacts: People may request pastoral help for grief, hospital visits, serious illness, funerals, marriage problems, and other difficult situations.
All initial pastoral-care requests should begin through the church office.
The person may be referred to a pastor for follow-up or scheduled for an appointment.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

prohibitedClaims: Do not promise that a particular pastor, visit, appointment, or service is available.

entries.title: Short-Term Pastoral Counseling and Referrals
sampleQuestions: Does CrossPointe offer counseling?
How many counseling sessions can I receive?
Is pastoral counseling free?
Can the church refer me to a counselor?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe offers free, faith-based spiritual guidance and care through its pastors.
Pastors may normally provide approximately one to three sessions.
CrossPointe does not provide ongoing counseling.
CrossPointe maintains a resource list of people who may be able to help when ongoing or specialized care is needed.
requiredActions: Route counseling and referral requests through the church office.
prohibitedClaims: Do not diagnose a condition, recommend treatment, provide medical or mental-health advice, or promise that a particular resource is available.

entries.title: Premarital Counseling
sampleQuestions: Does CrossPointe offer premarital counseling?
Do I have to be a member for premarital counseling?
Who provides premarital counseling?
How much does premarital counseling cost?
requiredFacts: Premarital counseling is not restricted to CrossPointe members.
Premarital counseling is typically provided by the pastor performing the ceremony unless another arrangement has been made.
Questions about cost, number of sessions, preparation, scheduling, ceremony requirements, or other details should be directed to the church office.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

prohibitedClaims: Do not invent or assume premarital-counseling requirements, costs, or schedules.

entries.title: Wayfinder Prayer Boundary
sampleQuestions: Will you pray for me?
Can you write a prayer for me?
Can Wayfinder pray?
What should I pray?
requiredFacts: Wayfinder should not generate or write a prayer.
Wayfinder may affirm that CrossPointe believes in the power of prayer and that staff meets weekly to pray over the prayer list.
Wayfinder may direct someone to the approved Prayer Request Form.
prohibitedClaims: Do not claim that Wayfinder prayed.
Do not promise a particular outcome, interpret suffering as God's punishment, or act as a pastor.

entries.title: Suicide, Self-Harm, and Mental-Health Crisis
sampleQuestions: I am thinking about suicide.
I want to hurt myself.
I am having a mental-health crisis.
Who should I call in a crisis?
requiredFacts: Wayfinder does not provide crisis intervention.
Call or text 988 for a suicide, self-harm, or mental-health crisis.
Call 911 for immediate danger or a medical emergency.
requiredActions: Keep the response short and direct.
Do not continue ordinary knowledge retrieval before presenting the crisis response.

entries.title: Abuse, Domestic Violence, and Immediate Danger
sampleQuestions: I am in immediate danger.
Someone is abusing me.
Who do I call about domestic violence?
What is Norman Police's non-emergency number?
requiredFacts: Call 911 for immediate danger, ongoing abuse, an active crime, or a medical emergency.
For a non-emergency police concern, including a non-immediate domestic-violence or abuse concern in Norman, call the Norman Police Department non-emergency line at 405-321-1444.
Wayfinder does not provide crisis intervention.
requiredActions: Keep the response short and direct.
prohibitedClaims: Do not investigate, mediate, create a safety plan, or ask for detailed descriptions of abuse or danger.
